name: sr_consent_explicit_grant
notes: The original VT-303 deterministic consent floor: a fresh un-consented tenant gets the enable-prompt, and the EXACT 'ACTIVATE TEAM' phrase grants consent via data_inputs_enable_handler (pre_filter Rule a2 — a zero-LLM exact-match path, independent of and unaffected by VT-583's fluid-affirm addition). Steps 1-2 hit direct handlers whose confirmation sends are not conversation_log-logged (transport quirk), so assert_no_silent is off there; step 3 is the load-bearing assertion. Run: `setup --owner-inputs false --name convo-harness-<id>` then `script <tenant> sr_consent_explicit_grant.json`.

steps.message: can you help me set up a WhatsApp offer for Diwali?
steps.note: Un-consented substantive message -> consent_required_handler sends the enable-prompt naming ACTIVATE TEAM. Not conversation_log-logged; run_status='completed' is the check.
The exact deterministic enable keyword — always honored regardless of any fluid-affirm logic. Fires data_inputs_enable_handler (owner_inputs -> true). Confirm send also not conversation_log-logged.

steps.message: ok, let's plan that Diwali offer now
steps.note: Load-bearing assertion: with consent granted, the brain must engage and produce a real, logged reply — never a re-ask of the consent prompt, never D1, never silence.
